title: Personality and behaviour
explanation:
Personality and behaviour

  • traits: reliable, stubborn, generous, thoughtful, selfish
  • behaviour: interrupt, complain, apologise, encourage
  • phrases: keep your word, jump to conclusions

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: You can trust her completely - she is very ____. | *reliable | jealous | clumsy
questions.feedback: Reliable people do what they promise.

questions.text: He never changes his mind. He is too ____. | *stubborn | generous | shy
It was very ____ of you to remember my birthday. | *thoughtful | formal | viral
Do not ____ to conclusions before you know the facts. | *jump | raise | broadcast
questions.feedback: Jump to conclusions is the expression.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: She always keeps her ____. If she promises something, she does it. | word
It was selfish of him not to ____ for being late. | apologise
Parents should ____ children to ask questions. | encourage
He tends to ____ when other people are speaking. | interrupt
A ____ person often shares time and money with others. | generous

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: She can be a bit ____ when things do not go her way. | *selfish | renewable | formal
Try not to ____ all the time; suggest a solution instead. | *complain | observe | hire
Good teachers are usually calm and ____. | *patient | viral | guilty
If you give your word, you should ____ it. | *keep | break in | take
